goal: Promote the `get_conventions` MCP tool from a `_meta.not_yet_implemented: true` stub to a real handler that reads project-specific coding conventions from the SQLite `conventions` table and returns them as a structured `_meta.conventions` array â€” empty list when no conventions have been extracted yet (no auto-extraction in this WO).
plan_summary: Add a new `Convention` struct + `list_conventions(category: Option<&str>) -> Result<Vec<Convention>, KnowledgeGraphError>` read helper to `crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs` backed by the existing `conventions` table (schema already created by `INIT_SQL` per WO-0011 and indexed by `idx_conventions_category`). Wire dispatch in `crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs::McpServer::handle_tools_call` so that when `self.kg.is_some() && name == "get_conventions"`, control falls through to a new `Self::handle_get_conventions(id, params, kg)` method (mirroring the `find_definition` gating pattern shipped in WO-0033); every other tool and `get_conventions` when no KG is attached keep the `_meta.not_yet_implemented: true` stub so the phase-1 invariant #9 is preserved for all 21 other tools. `handle_get_conventions` extracts an optional `arguments.category` (string) from the JSON-RPC params, calls `list_conventions(category)`, and returns the MCP `tools/call` envelope shape `result: {_meta: {tool: "get_conventions", source: "kg", count, category, conventions: [...]}, content: [{type: "text", text: "â€¦"}], isError: false}`. Empty list when the table has no rows is a **non-error** response per P1-W4-F10's description ("empty list if none yet extracted"). The acceptance test `server::test_get_conventions_tool` lives as a module-level `#[cfg(test)] #[tokio::test]` item in `server.rs` (same scope as `test_find_definition_tool` / `test_all_22_tools_registered`) and (a) opens a temp SQLite KG, (b) inserts two real rows into `conventions` via a new `KnowledgeGraph::insert_convention` write helper (one `category='naming'`, one `category='error_handling'`), (c) builds `McpServer::with_knowledge_graph(Arc::clone(&kg))`, (d) calls `handle_line` with a `tools/call` request for `get_conventions` (both with and without `arguments.category` filter), and (e) asserts the response `_meta.conventions` contains exactly the inserted rows (filtered correctly when a category is passed, returns both when unfiltered), plus a separate negative test `test_get_conventions_tool_empty` that asserts `_meta.count == 0` and `_meta.conventions == []` when the table is empty (the "none yet extracted" path).
scope_in: New `Convention` struct in `crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs` (or a neighbouring types module) with fields: `id: Option<i64>`, `category: String`, `pattern: String`, `examples: Option<String>`, `counter_examples: Option<String>`, `confidence: f64`, `evidence_count: i64`, `t_ingested_at: String`, `last_verified: Option<String>`, `scope: String`. Derive `Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize`. Rustdoc each field referencing master-plan Â§12.1 lines 1172-1182.

New public `insert_convention(&mut self, convention: &Convention) -> Result<i64, KnowledgeGraphError>` write helper on `KnowledgeGraph`. Uses a single `INSERT INTO conventions (category, pattern, examples, counter_examples, confidence, evidence_count, scope) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)` â€” the `t_ingested_at` column is populated by the schema-level `DEFAULT (datetime('now'))`. Returns the new rowid. `#[tracing::instrument]` span `ucil.core.kg.insert_convention`. Rustdoc refs master-plan Â§12.1 row `conventions`.

New public `list_conventions(&self, category: Option<&str>) -> Result<Vec<Convention>, KnowledgeGraphError>` read helper. When `category.is_some()`, uses `SELECT id, category, pattern, examples, counter_examples, confidence, evidence_count, t_ingested_at, last_verified, scope FROM conventions WHERE category = ?1 ORDER BY id ASC`; when `None`, drops the `WHERE` clause. Returns `Vec<Convention>`, never `Err` on empty result set (empty vec is the empty-table signal). `#[tracing::instrument]` span `ucil.core.kg.list_conventions`. Mirrors the existing `list_relations_by_source` read helper pattern (rowid order, `Option<_>` columns mapped with `rusqlite::Row::get`).

Unit tests under `#[cfg(test)]` inside `knowledge_graph.rs` (the existing test module) covering: (a) round-trip â€” insert two rows in different categories, `list_conventions(None)` returns both in id order; (b) category filter â€” `list_conventions(Some("naming"))` returns only the naming row; (c) unknown category â€” `list_conventions(Some("nonexistent"))` returns empty vec (not Err); (d) empty table â€” `list_conventions(None)` on a fresh KG returns `Ok(vec![])`. All tests use a `tempfile::TempDir` + `KnowledgeGraph::open`; no mocks.

Extend `crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs::McpServer::handle_tools_call` with a second dispatch branch: when `name == "get_conventions" && self.kg.is_some()`, call `Self::handle_get_conventions(id, params, self.kg.as_ref().unwrap())`. The existing `find_definition` branch is untouched. All 20 remaining tools fall through to the stub as before; phase-1 invariant #9 preserved.

New `handle_get_conventions(id: &Value, params: &Value, kg: &Arc<Mutex<KnowledgeGraph>>) -> Value` method in `server.rs` that: (1) extracts `arguments.category` as `Option<&str>` â€” a missing or null `category` means "no filter" (NOT an error); (2) if `category` is present but non-string, returns JSON-RPC error `-32602` (invalid params); (3) acquires the KG lock, calls `list_conventions(category)`, releases the lock; (4) projects each `Convention` onto a JSON object `{id, category, pattern, examples, counter_examples, confidence, evidence_count, t_ingested_at, last_verified, scope}`; (5) returns the structured `tools/call` envelope with `_meta: {tool: "get_conventions", source: "kg", count, category, conventions}` where `count` is the length and `category` echoes the filter (null when unfiltered); (6) the `content[0].text` is a short human-readable summary (e.g. `"2 conventions"` or `"no conventions yet"` for the empty case); (7) `isError` is always `false` â€” the "empty table" case is a successful no-results response, not an error, per the P1-W4-F10 description.

Dedicated response/error helpers mirroring the `find_definition` module structure: a private `get_conventions_found_response(id, category, conventions_json)` builder and a private `read_conventions(kg, category) -> Result<Vec<Convention>, GetConventionsReadError>` function that encapsulates the mutex-lock + KG call so the handler stays under `clippy::too_many_lines`. Reuse the existing `jsonrpc_error` helper for JSON-RPC error envelopes; reuse `JSONRPC_VERSION` const.

Add the acceptance test `test_get_conventions_tool` as a module-level `#[cfg(test)] #[tokio::test]` item in `server.rs`, placed adjacent to `test_find_definition_tool`, so the nextest selector `server::test_get_conventions_tool` resolves per the WO-0006 test-selector rule and DEC-0007. Test body: (a) `tempfile::TempDir` + `KnowledgeGraph::open`; (b) `kg.insert_convention` two rows (one `category='naming'`, pattern `'snake_case for functions'`; one `category='error_handling'`, pattern `'thiserror on all library errors'`); (c) wrap in `Arc::new(Mutex::new(kg))`; (d) build `McpServer::with_knowledge_graph(kg)`; (e) invoke `server.handle_line(r#"{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"get_conventions","arguments":{}}}"#)` and assert `_meta.count == 2`, `_meta.category == null`, `_meta.conventions` has two entries with correct `category`/`pattern` round-tripped; (f) invoke again with `"arguments":{"category":"naming"}` and assert `_meta.count == 1`, `_meta.category == "naming"`, `_meta.conventions[0].pattern == "snake_case for functions"`; (g) every assertion uses real data â€” no mocks of SQLite or serde_json.

Supplementary `#[cfg(test)]` tests adjacent to the main acceptance test: (i) `test_get_conventions_tool_empty` â€” fresh KG with zero conventions, assert `_meta.count == 0`, `_meta.conventions == []`, `isError == false`, and `content[0].text` says something like `"no conventions yet"`; (ii) `test_get_conventions_tool_no_kg_returns_stub` â€” `McpServer::new()` (no KG attached), assert the response still returns `_meta.not_yet_implemented == true` (phase-1 invariant #9 preservation when the server has no KG bound); (iii) `test_get_conventions_tool_non_string_category` â€” `arguments.category: 123`, assert JSON-RPC `error.code == -32602`.

Rustdoc on every new public item (`Convention`, `insert_convention`, `list_conventions`, `handle_get_conventions`, private helpers with `pub(crate)` visibility) referencing: master-plan Â§3.2 row 7 (`get_conventions`), master-plan Â§12.1 lines 1172-1182 (`conventions` table schema + category enum comment), master-plan Â§18 Phase 1 Week 4 line 1751 (CEQP universal schema already present), phase-1 CLAUDE.md invariant #9 (other 21 tools keep the stub), and feature-list:P1-W4-F10 frozen selector `-p ucil-daemon server::test_get_conventions_tool`.

Errors surfaced via the existing `KnowledgeGraphError` enum (no new variant needed â€” the generic `Sqlite` variant covers connection/statement failures). No new error type in `ucil-daemon`; reuse `jsonrpc_error` for -32602 / -32603 envelopes.
`ready-for-review.md` at `ucil-build/work-orders/0034-ready-for-review.md` summarising the diff, commits, and acceptance outcome; per the WO-0033 precedent.
scope_out: Automatic convention EXTRACTION from parsed source (e.g. frequency-analysis of naming patterns in the fixture projects) â€” the master plan reserves that for the G5 group-fusion layer in Phase 2+, and the P1-W4-F10 description explicitly says "empty list if none yet extracted". This WO only wires the READ side of the pipeline; writes are exercised by the acceptance test's `insert_convention` call, not by an automated extractor.

Hot-staging convention signals (`hot_convention_signals` table / `stage_hot_convention_signal` â€” already in master-plan Â§12.1 and the KG CRUD for that path was covered by an earlier WO). This WO's writes target the cold `conventions` table directly; the hotâ†’warmâ†’cold promotion loop is a Phase-3 concern (see master-plan Â§12.2).

CEQP universal-parameter semantic processing (`reason`, `task_goal`, `files_in_context`, `non_obvious_info_agent_has`) â€” the schema advertises them (P1-W4-F06 passing); this WO reads them from `arguments` but does NOT act on them. Applying `reason` for enrichment is a Â§18 Phase 2+ concern.
Serena or Salsa integration in the `get_conventions` read path. The G3 (conventions) fusion layer is master-plan Â§17 row G3 and is Phase-3 scope.

Handler wiring for the remaining 20 MCP tools still stubbed after WO-0033 (`understand_code` = P1-W4-F09, `search_code` = P1-W5-F09, `find_references` = P1-W5-F07, etc.) â€” each is a separate feature with its own WO.
Schema migrations beyond what `INIT_SQL` already creates. The `conventions` table + its `idx_conventions_category` index exist via the WO-0011 schema seed.
Changes to any file under `tests/fixtures/**`, `scripts/gate/**`, `scripts/flip-feature.sh`, `ucil-build/feature-list.json`, `ucil-build/feature-list.schema.json`, or the master plan.
Changes to the 22-tool catalog or its `ucil_tools()` builder â€” the catalog is frozen under WO-0010's acceptance test.
Changes to `McpServer::serve` control flow beyond the single new dispatch branch in `handle_tools_call`.
acceptance: `get_conventions` MCP tool returns real rows from the `conventions` table â€” filtered by optional `arguments.category` â€” driven by a real `KnowledgeGraph::open` + `insert_convention` sequence on a temp SQLite in the acceptance test.
Empty-table path returns `_meta.count == 0`, `_meta.conventions == []`, and `isError == false` â€” matching P1-W4-F10's "empty list if none yet extracted" contract.
The frozen acceptance selector `server::test_get_conventions_tool` resolves under `cargo nextest` with the mainline executor profile per DEC-0007 and the WO-0006 test-selector rule.
The 22-tool catalog stays wire-compatible: `server::test_all_22_tools_registered` stays green and all 20 still-stubbed tools keep returning `_meta.not_yet_implemented: true`.
`find_definition` regression safety: `server::test_find_definition_tool` stays green â€” the new dispatch branch does not shadow or alter the existing `find_definition` gating.
No regressions across the workspace: `cargo nextest run --workspace` and `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` stay green. `cargo fmt --all --check` is clean.

Zero new stubs introduced: `grep -nE 'todo!\\(|unimplemented!\\(|NotImplementedError|raise NotImplementedError'` across the WO diff yields zero hits. Phase-1 invariant #9 is preserved for the 20 tools that still stub.
Every public item added has rustdoc; `cargo doc -p ucil-daemon --no-deps` and `cargo doc -p ucil-core --no-deps` emit zero warnings/errors.

Commit cadence per CLAUDE.md â‰¤50-LOC-per-commit target: (1) `Convention` struct + rustdoc; (2) `insert_convention` write helper; (3) `list_conventions` read helper; (4) KG unit tests for both helpers; (5) `handle_get_conventions` happy path + dispatch wiring in `handle_tools_call`; (6) error paths (non-string category â†’ -32602, empty-table â†’ non-error); (7) `test_get_conventions_tool` + negative tests; (8) rustdoc polish + `ready-for-review.md`. 7-8 commits total.

`reality-check.sh` (verifier step 6b) passes: stashing the `handle_get_conventions` method + dispatch wiring causes `test_get_conventions_tool` to FAIL; popping restores GREEN. Proves the test exercises real code, not a mock.
`ready-for-review.md` exists at `ucil-build/work-orders/0034-ready-for-review.md` and summarises the implementation + acceptance outcome.
